description: Adv4 voice: TB case dictated with disfluencies + restart phrase + acronym. Lookup table has TB alias (per adv2_acronym_only_tb). Tests tier-1 lookup robustness on dictated prose.
user:
Patient: Marcus Reed
Gender: M
DOB: 1964-03-22
Location: Newark, NJ 07102
Facility: University Hospital Newark (NPI: 1234567840)
Encounter: 2026-04-18
This is a, uh, 62-year-old male, recent immigrant from a high-prevalence country, presenting with — sorry, I should say presenting for — about eight weeks now of productive cough with hemoptysis, drenching night sweats, and roughly fifteen pounds of unintentional weight loss. Um, household contact with confirmed active TB. Sputum AFB smear came back positive, and the Xpert MTB/RIF was, uh, MTB detected, no rifampin resistance. Assessment is active pulmonary TB. We started him on the standard four-drug regimen — isoniazid, rifampin, pyrazinamide, ethambutol — per the WHO protocol. Yeah, that's everything for now.
Vitals: Temp 38.0C, HR 96, RR 22, SpO2 95%, BP 118

description: Adv4 voice: Valley fever case dictated in colloquial form. Coccidioidomycosis NOT in lookup but IS in RAG (per adv3_valley_fever_rag). Tests RAG-tier handling of dictated informal disease name.
user:
Patient: Elena Vasquez
Gender: F
DOB: 1979-11-14
Location: Bakersfield, CA 93301
Facility: Kern Medical (NPI: 1234567841)
Encounter: 2026-04-22
Um, 45-year-old female construction worker, working out in the San Joaquin Valley, presents with — three weeks of dry cough now, fatigue, low-grade fevers, and she's got that, uh, erythema nodosum on her shins. So this is, you know, the classic valley fever picture. Uh, the IgM by EIA came back positive for, sorry, Coccidioides immitis IgM, and her chest x-ray shows a right middle lobe infiltrate. Started fluconazole 400 milligrams by mouth daily, planning three to six months of therapy depending on response.
Vitals: Temp 37.9C, HR 84, RR 16, SpO2 97%, BP 124

description: Adv4 voice: H5N1 dictated colloquially as 'bird flu thing' with later formal name. Lookup HAS H5N1 (per adv2_h5n1_avian_flu). Tests dictated alias resolution.
user:
Patient: Wei-Chen Lim
Gender: M
DOB: 1971-08-09
Location: Modesto, CA 95350
Facility: Stanislaus County Public Health (NPI: 1234567842)
Encounter: 2026-04-15
OK so, this is a 54-year-old guy, occupational exposure on a poultry farm — they had a confirmed H5N1 outbreak, that, uh, that bird flu thing in the news. He's coming in with high fever, 39.6, severe conjunctivitis, myalgia, shortness of breath for, um, four days now. The H5 RNA NAA on the nasopharyngeal swab came back detected. Diagnosis is avian influenza A H5N1 infection. We started oseltamivir 75 milligrams twice daily for five days.
Vitals: Temp 39.6C, HR 110, RR 26, SpO2 91%, BP 118

description: Adv4 voice: Strep throat dictated with restart phrase ('the patient — sorry, I mean'). Strep IS in lookup (43878008, per adv_prose_narrative_strep). Tests restart-phrase resilience in tier-1.
user:
Patient: Sean O'Brien
Gender: M
DOB: 1990-02-11
Location: Buffalo, NY 14202
Facility: Buffalo General Medical Center (NPI: 1234567843)
Encounter: 2026-04-20
Uh, this is a healthy 35 — sorry, I mean 36-year-old male, presents with two days of severe sore throat, odynophagia, and subjective fevers. Bilateral tonsillar exudates on exam, anterior cervical lymphadenopathy, no cough. Rapid antigen detection test for, um, group A Streptococcus came back positive at point-of-care. So this is strep pharyngitis. We started him on amoxicillin 500 milligrams by mouth three times a day for ten days, and supportive care with acetaminophen and saltwater gargles.
Vitals: Temp 38.6C, HR 92, RR 16, SpO2 99%, BP 122
